description.en: Named for the Lüneburg Heath, whose thin, sandy soil favors buckwheat - locally called Heidekorn - over wheat. This sourdough loaf folds a little of that buckwheat and wholegrain rye into a mostly-wheat dough: rustic crust, nutty, earthy crumb.
description.de: Benannt nach der Lüneburger Heide, deren sandige Böden eher Buchweizen – dort 'Heidekorn' genannt – als Weizen tragen. Dieses Sauerteigbrot mischt etwas Buchweizen und Roggenvollkornmehl in einen überwiegend weizenen Teig: rustikale Kruste, nussig-erdige Krume.
description.es: Llamado así por el brezal de Lüneburg, cuyo suelo arenoso favorece el trigo sarraceno, allí llamado 'Heidekorn', antes que el trigo. Este pan de masa madre mezcla algo de ese grano y centeno integral en una masa de trigo: corteza rústica, miga a nuez y tierra.
description.fr: Nommé d'après la lande de Lüneburg, dont le sol sableux favorise le sarrasin, appelé localement 'Heidekorn', plutôt que le blé. Ce pain au levain mêle un peu de ce sarrasin et de seigle complet à une pâte surtout froment: croûte rustique, mie noisette.

origin.note: Buckwheat was historically called 'Heidekorn' (heath grain) because it tolerates the Heath's poor, sandy soil far better than wheat or rye.

ingredients.note: Weizenmehl Type 550, split between sourdough and main dough

keeping.note: Sourdough tang and the rye-buckwheat blend keep it moist for several days; freezes well sliced.

method.title: Build the sourdough
method.summary: Mix flour, water and a little starter; ferment covered overnight at room temperature until ripe and domed.

method.title: Mix the dough
method.summary: Combine the ripe sourdough with the remaining wheat flour, the rye and buckwheat wholegrain flours, water, salt and a small amount of yeast; mix into a smooth dough.

method.title: Bulk ferment
method.summary: Cover and let rise until visibly expanded, folding once or twice to build strength.

method.title: Divide and shape
method.summary: Divide in two, shape each piece into a tight round, and set seam-up in floured proofing baskets.

method.title: Final proof
method.summary: Let rise covered until nicely domed and springy.

method.title: Bake
method.summary: Turn out onto a hot stone, bake with steam at high heat, then drop the temperature and continue until deeply browned, about 45 minutes total.

method.title: Cool
method.summary: Cool completely on a rack before slicing.

source.adaptation: Method paraphrased; the source does not mention a rolled-oats topping, so none is included; overall timing outside the stated bake time is estimated from typical practice for this style.
